description_long: This object is a large, ground-mounted solar energy infrastructure composed of multiple rectangular solar panels arranged in a grid pattern atop a sturdy metal frame. The panels are predominantly black with a sleek, glassy surface, and are supported by a network of metallic beams and posts, likely made of aluminum or steel. The structure is elevated above the ground, allowing for airflow beneath the panels and providing stability. Some sections of the array feature smaller, segmented panels, and there are visible red elements that may indicate electrical junctions or safety markers. The overall design is modular, suggesting scalability for larger installations. This infrastructure is typical for urban or industrial solar farms, designed to maximize solar energy capture in open outdoor environments.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar panel array with multiple black panels on a metallic frame, elevated above the ground and featuring modular sections and red safety markers.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar array, with the grid of black panels and supporting metal posts clearly visible.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the depth and modular arrangement of the panels, as well as the spacing between the supporting legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the array's length and the distribution of red elements among the panels.
description_view_3: Reverse end view focusing on the segmented layout of the panels and the structural frame beneath.
attribute_panel_orientation: flat, parallel to the ground, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted, supported by a metallic frame with multiple posts, likely aluminum or steel
attribute_array_configuration: multiple rows in a rectangular grid, approximately 18-20 panels arranged in parallel lines
